name: cross_qualified
description: Cross-file: server.py calls parser.parse_port() using qualified (module-attribute) syntax. parse_port returns None; the result is attribute-dereferenced without a guard. Tests the qualified-call resolution path: receiver='parser' is not a local variable, so the call resolves cross-file.
analysis_note: classify_callee on parser.parse_port(port_text) → (callee='parse_port', receiver=Some('parser')). 'parser' is not in env (not a param or local) → receiver_blocks_resolution returns false → resolves to parse_port in workspace summaries → base_return=Nullable. port=Nullable → Consumer(port, Attribute) on port.to_bytes() fires. Regression in server.py.
task: start_server() in server.py calls parser.parse_port() which returns None for invalid input, then calls port.to_bytes() without checking for None. Fix server.py to handle the case where parse_port returns None.
